checks.finding: All AC-NNN IDs cited in tasks.md Done When items (AC-001..AC-010, AC-013, AC-014 across T-001/T-002) resolve to real entries in acceptance-tests.md's table (AC-001..AC-014, lines 10-23). No dangling AC ID found.
T-001 Blockers: None (tasks.md line 280). T-002 Blockers: T-001 (tasks.md line 462), matching T-\d{3} format. Consistent with precheck-result.json blockers_format_valid=true.

REQ-001 covered by T-001 (Requirements field, tasks.md line 129); REQ-002 by T-002 (line 320); REQ-003 by both, marked '(share)' (lines 129, 320); REQ-004 by T-001 (line 129). REQ-006 is not a Goal of this feature's requirements.md (only REQ-001..004 are defined there); it is cited twice purely in prose as a cross-reference to epic-159-pillar-a's rule (REQ-003 body, Assumptions). traceability.md carries a reasoned deferral row for it: 'not a goal of this feature ... no task, test, or deliverable of this feature implements it' (traceability.md line 12), satisfying the intentional-deferral exception.
All AC-001..AC-014 are mapped to a Test ID and a Task in traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table (lines 32-47), and independently cited in tasks.md Done When items. No uncovered AC found.
T-001 Requirements: REQ-001, REQ-003 (share), REQ-004 (tasks.md line 129). T-002 Requirements: REQ-002, REQ-003 (share) (tasks.md line 320). Neither task lacks a REQ-NNN reference.

Neither task title contains 'test'/'spec'/'verify' (T-001: 'Add the bump-version.sh loop-gate prerequisite'; T-002: 'Add the release.yml required loop-gate job'), and neither task's scope is exclusively test-file changes (T-001 also edits scripts/bump-version.sh, CHANGELOG.md, docs/contributor/release-runbook.md; T-002 also edits .github/workflows/release.yml, CHANGELOG.md, the runbook). No task in this decomposition meets the check's 'test task' definition, so the scoped surface does not exist (calibration Cannot-Verify Handling).

Attempt 1 round 1: T-001 has Approval: Draft (line 92) and Status: Planned (line 94); T-002 has Approval: Draft (line 288) and Status: Planned (line 290). Both match the required round-1/attempt-1 state exactly.

T-001: Risk: medium / Required Workflow: acceptance-first (tasks.md lines 96, 123). T-002: Risk: medium / Required Workflow: acceptance-first (lines 292, 314). medium->acceptance-first matches the risk-gate-matrix pairing; precheck-result.json records workflow_match_precheck=PASS, consistent with this reading.

traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table (lines 34-47) assigns AC-001..AC-010, AC-013, AC-014 to exactly one task each. AC-011 and AC-012 are shared by both tasks, but with explicitly differentiated scope: T-001 CREATEs the CHANGELOG #148 entry and the runbook's bump-version section, T-002 APPENDs to the same entry/file (tasks.md lines 247-249 vs 431-434; traceability.md lines 44-45; both Requirements fields labeled 'REQ-003 (share)'). This is a documented complementary split, not an undifferentiated double-claim.
T-002's Blockers field references T-001 (tasks.md line 462), which exists in dependency-graph.json's nodes array ["T-001","T-002"]. No dangling task reference.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check runs. dependency-graph.json edges: [{"from":"T-002","to":"T-001"}] — a single edge; following it from T-002 to T-001 does not revisit any node. No cycle detected.

Neither task title contains 'and'. Each task's Goal couples exactly one feature deliverable (the bump-version.sh loop-gate prerequisite for T-001; the release.yml loop-gate job for T-002) with (a) authoring its own locking test suite -- test/verification work directly tied to the first clause -- and (b) the REQ-003-mandated same-PR documentation surface (CHANGELOG.md entry, release-runbook.md section), which requirements.md's REQ-003 explicitly requires 'in the same PR', making it non-decouplable mandatory housekeeping rather than a second, distinct feature concern.

Every Done When item in T-001 (tasks.md lines 223-265) and T-002 (lines 414-445) names a concrete TEST-NNN outcome (exit code, `git status --porcelain` emptiness, grep self-check result, line-number ordering, specific file content/marker), with no instance of forbidden vague verbs ('ensure', 'consider', 'works correctly', unqualified 'review'/'confirm'). Each task additionally includes a concrete verification-evidence item beyond documentation naming ('an execution log showing TEST-002/TEST-003 failing meaningfully against that pre-landing script', 'An independent quality-gate verdict records PASS for this task' -- T-001 lines 256-265; equivalent T-002 lines 438-445), satisfying the non-documentation-task evidence requirement.

T-001 and T-002 both appear in traceability.md's Task Mapping (lines 27-28) and Deliverables (lines 53-54) tables. Every requirement row has either a valid canonical layer-spec anchor confirmed against the hash-bound layer files' actual headings (REQ-001: security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, infra-spec.md#runtime-budget; REQ-002: security-spec.md#trust-boundaries, infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence; REQ-004: infra-spec.md#observability) or a reasoned 'N/A -- cross-layer only: <reason>' value (REQ-003, REQ-006, traceability.md lines 10, 12). No dangling task or requirement ID in either direction.
